user_message: Help me build migration architecture for Woodgrove Bank. We had a deep-dive last month -- need the follow-up.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Rachel Berg <rachel.berg@woodgroveban.com>
To: Jennifer Walsh <jennifer.walsh@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-07 17:41 EST
Subject: Re: migration architecture Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with migration architecture. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Jennifer Walsh <jennifer.walsh@microsoft.com>
To: Rachel Berg <rachel.berg@woodgroveban.com>
Date: 2024-01-21 10:52 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Woodgrove Bank migration architecture

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with migration architecture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - Woodgrove Bank
Date: 2024-02-27
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Rachel Berg (Woodgrove Bank), Jennifer Walsh (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Rachel Berg (Woodgrove Bank): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is migration architecture.

[00:08:00] Rachel Berg (Woodgrove Bank): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:11:00] Rachel Berg (Woodgrove Bank): The timeline for data residency is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:16:00] Jennifer Walsh (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:19:00] Jennifer Walsh (Microsoft): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:24:00] Rachel Berg (Woodgrove Bank): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:27:00] Rachel Berg (Woodgrove Bank): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:31:00] Rachel Berg (Woodgrove Bank): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

[00:34:00] Rachel Berg (Woodgrove Bank): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

[00:37:00] Rachel Berg (Woodgrove Bank): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Perfect execution of the golden-path workflow with all artifacts complete and actionable
Score 3: Core output correct but missing polish or one secondary artifact
Score 1: Fails to produce expected output for a clean, unambiguous request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: All evidence correctly sourced and attributed; no hallucination
Score 3: Minor attribution gap but no fabrication
Score 1: Evidence fabricated or significantly misattributed
decision_anchors: Score 5: All decisions correct for this straightforward scenario
Score 3: Primary decision correct; minor secondary issue
Score 1: Primary decision wrong despite clear inputs

guardrail_checks: Clean golden-path scenario -- all guardrails should pass trivially
Must not over-complicate a straightforward request
